Something large moving along the ridge after dark
Fresh tracks where there shouldn't be any
A shape watching from the tree line at dusk
Claw marks gouged into the bark about eight feet up
A dead animal left half-eaten by the watering hole
Something with too many legs skittering across the old stones
A low growl from underneath the footbridge, but nothing visible
Drag marks leading from the livestock pen into the tall grass
Two sets of eyes reflecting the firelight just past the clearing
A hunter came back white as chalk and wouldn't say what he saw
Birds circling over the hillside for hours, never landing
Something tall standing perfectly still between the trees
Prints in the mud the size of a dinner plate
Fur caught on the fence posts, dark and coarse like nothing local
The dogs refused to cross the stream, kept whimpering and pulling back
A sound like breathing coming from inside the collapsed mine
The shepherd's boy swears he saw something walk upright through the meadow
Deep scratches on the outside of the storage shed door
Whatever it was, it moved too fast for its size
A trail of broken branches leading up into the hills, shoulder height
Something splashing in the pond well after midnight
Livestock found huddled in the corner of the pen, trembling
An outline against the sky on the ridge, gone when the clouds shifted
Three deer carcasses piled together near the old foundation
A woman gathering herbs said something followed her along the tree line
Feathers everywhere by the coop, but no blood and no bodies
The watchman heard hooves on stone, but the road was empty
A burrow dug under the wall, wide enough for a person to crawl through
Something howled from the direction of the ruins, long and deliberate
The fish in the lower creek have all gone, like they fled downstream
A shape moving through the fog, low to the ground and quick
Tooth marks on the well rope, clean through the fibers
Whatever killed those rabbits didn't eat them, just left them arranged in a line
A farmhand found a den in the hillside, freshly dug and lined with bones
The horses won't go near the north trail anymore
Muddy handprints on the outside of the shutters, but the fingers were wrong
A trader said he saw something crouched on the old bridge at dawn
An awful stench rolling down from the caves when the wind shifts
The goats got loose and all ran the same direction, toward the village center
Scratch marks on the inside of the abandoned watchtower door
